skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
skill.summary: Execute implementation plans in batches with human review checkpoints for safe, controlled development
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3 and 81 as HIGH risk. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the scanner incorrectly matched on the word 'description' in the YAML frontmatter. There is no cryptographic code or algorithms in this skill. This is a simple workflow management skill that safely executes implementation plans in batches with mandatory human review checkpoints between batches. The skill has appropriate safety mechanisms: it stops when blocked, asks for clarification rather than guessing, and requires using the finishing-a-development-branch sub-skill for completion.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged 'description' field content as cryptographic algorithm. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain plain English text describing the skill's purpose. No cryptographic functions, algorithms, or encoding operations are present in this file.

content.value_statement: This skill helps Claude Code safely execute implementation plans by breaking work into batches and requiring human review between each batch, preventing costly mistakes and ensuring alignment with goals.
actual_capabilities: Load and critically review implementation plans before execution
Execute tasks in configurable batches (default 3 tasks per batch)
Report progress and verification output after each batch
Stop execution and ask for clarification when blocked or uncertain
Integrate with finishing-a-development-branch skill for completion
limitations: Requires a pre-written implementation plan to function
Cannot create plans - only executes existing ones
Depends on human partner for feedback between batches
May not handle plans with ambiguous or missing steps

use_cases.title: Multi-Step Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Execute a complex feature implementation plan that spans multiple files and requires testing at each stage
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on features that need staged implementation

use_cases.title: Refactoring Projects
use_cases.description: Safely refactor legacy code by following a plan with checkpoints to verify each change works before proceeding
use_cases.target_user: Engineers maintaining or modernizing existing codebases

use_cases.title: Onboarding to New Codebases
use_cases.description: Follow an onboarding plan that introduces a new codebase incrementally with review at each learning milestone
use_cases.target_user: New team members getting familiar with project structure

prompt_templates.title: Basic Plan Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to implement the plan in plan.md. Start with the first batch of tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new plan execution from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Continue After Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the feedback provided and then execute the next batch of tasks using the executing-plans skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: Continuing plan execution after receiving human feedback

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Load the plan from plan.md, review it critically, and raise any concerns before starting execution.

prompt_templates.title: Complete With Finishing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: The plan is complete. Use the finishing-a-development-branch skill to verify tests, present options, and execute the final choice.

output_examples.input: Use the executing-plans skill to implement the plan in plan.md
output_examples.output:
I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan.

I've loaded and reviewed the plan. Creating my task list now.

**Batch 1 (Tasks 1-3):**
- [ ] Task 1: Create new component file
- [ ] Task 2: Add basic structure
- [ ] Task 3: Implement initial functionality

Ready for feedback.

output_examples.input: Feedback: Looks good, continue with next batch
output_examples.output:
**Batch 2 (Tasks 4-6):**
- [ ] Task 4: Add unit tests
- [ ] Task 5: Verify tests pass
- [ ] Task 6: Update documentation

Ready for feedback.

best_practices: Always review the plan critically before starting - identify gaps or concerns early
Follow the plan steps exactly and run all verifications specified
Stop immediately and ask for clarification when you encounter blockers or unclear instructions
anti_patterns: Skipping the critical review step and executing blindly
Continuing through blockers without asking for help
Skipping verification steps to save time
Executing all tasks at once without waiting for feedback

faq.question: What is the executing-plans skill?
faq.answer: It is a workflow skill that helps execute implementation plans in batches with human review checkpoints between each batch.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you have a written implementation plan and want controlled execution with regular feedback opportunities.

faq.question: How many tasks execute per batch?
faq.answer: The default is 3 tasks per batch, but this can be adjusted based on the plan size and complexity.

faq.question: What happens if I encounter a blocker?
faq.answer: Stop immediately and ask for clarification. Do not guess or force through blockers.

faq.question: Do I need a separate plan file?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires a pre-written plan file (like plan.md) that contains the implementation steps.

faq.question: How does this skill handle plan changes?
faq.answer: If your human partner updates the plan based on your feedback, return to the review step before continuing execution.
